content_hints.description: Bridge Nine's quarters -- a lean-to of salvaged bridge timber and storm-beaten canvas, barely large enough for twenty men to sleep shoulder to shoulder. The branded men here wear their shash glyphs like uniforms. Someone has scratched a tally of bridge runs on the support beam: forty-seven marks, twelve crossed out. The crossed-out ones are the runs everyone came back from.

npcs.name: Sergeant Talno
npcs.dialogue: You are Bridge Nine now. That means you carry the bridge, you run when told, and you do not die unless I give permission. I have not given permission to anyone yet. That is why we still have twenty men.

npcs.name: Pav
npcs.dialogue: Do not listen to the sergeant's bluster. He cried last time we lost someone. In private, sure, but the canvas walls do not hide much.

event_triggers: On entry: A branded man looks up from sharpening a salvaged knife and nods -- not a greeting, but an acknowledgment that you exist, which is apparently the highest compliment here

content_hints.description: The Mess Hall is a generous name for a tarpaulin stretched over four poles with a fire pit underneath. The stew tastes of whatever animal wandered too close to camp that day, thickened with Soulcast grain that has the texture and flavor of wet chalk. Men eat it without complaint. Complaining takes energy better spent on surviving.

npcs.name: Cook Shen
npcs.dialogue: It is food. It fills the belly. If you want flavor, become a lighteyes. If you want seconds, bring me something worth cooking. A chull steak, maybe. Or a cremling -- I am not proud.

npcs.name: Maps
npcs.dialogue: They call me Maps because I drew the plateau runs from memory. Forty-seven runs, every chasm crossing, every Parshendi ambush point. The officers do not care. Bridge crews are not supposed to think. But Bridge Nine thinks anyway.

event_triggers: On entry: Maps unfurls a hand-drawn chart on the mess table -- every plateau run Bridge Nine has survived, marked with the names of men who did not

content_hints.description: The Training Yard is a patch of bare stone behind the barracks where Sergeant Talno drills the crew before dawn. Bridge carriers need legs like iron and shoulders that can bear four hundred pounds at a dead sprint. The training is brutal and effective -- the men who survive it survive the runs. The correlation is not coincidental.

npcs.name: Hobber
npcs.dialogue: Lift with the legs, not the back. And when the arrows start, do not flinch. Flinching slows you down, and the man behind you trips, and then the bridge drops, and then everyone dies. So do not flinch.

event_triggers: On entry: Talno is making two men sprint while carrying a log between them. The log has 'BRIDGE' carved into it. The metaphor is not subtle.

content_hints.description: The Lashing Post stands at the camp's crossroads -- a weathered wooden pillar stained dark at waist height from a decade of punishments. Today it is empty. The guards say that is a good day. The bridge crews say that means tomorrow will be worse, because discipline always catches up.

npcs.name: Guard Hav
npcs.dialogue: Keep your brands visible and your mouth shut. The highprince does not tolerate dissent from bridge crews. The last man who spoke up is still tied to the medical tent. You do not want to know what for.

event_triggers: On entry: A man stumbles past, fresh welts on his back, heading toward the barracks. No one helps him. Not because they do not care, but because helping marks you as sympathetic, and sympathy is a punishable offense.

content_hints.description: The Sergeant's Tent is the only fully enclosed structure in the bridge crew compound -- a double-walled canvas shelter with an actual cot, a locked chest, and a map of tomorrow's plateau run pinned to the center pole. The luxury is relative, but among branded men, a tent with walls is a palace.

npcs.name: Sergeant Talno
npcs.dialogue: Tomorrow's run is a long one -- seven chasms, two contested plateaus. The gemheart scouts say the chrysalis is ripe. That means the Parshendi know too. I need every man sharp. No injuries, no arguments, no incidents. Can Bridge Nine manage that?

event_triggers: On entry: Talno pins a new run map to the pole. His hand trembles once, briefly, before steadying. He does not look up.

content_hints.description: The Camp Gate is a gap in the perimeter stakes where bridge crews are mustered for their runs. Beyond it, the Shattered Plains stretch under a sky that never looks the same twice -- pale amber before highstorm, deep blue after, and right now a bruised purple that promises nothing good.

npcs.name: Sutler Goshel
npcs.dialogue: Knives, bandages, dried meat. One sphere each, no haggling. I do not extend credit to bridge crews. Nothing personal -- it is a poor investment.

event_triggers: On entry: The assembly horn sounds. Bridge crews form up at the gate in practiced silence. Somewhere, a gemheart is growing, and two nations are about to fight for it.
